campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and spending activity. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, such as bike lanes, public transit, and pedestrian pathways.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment and cash flow reports
City treasurer's monthly financial overview
Multimodal transportation improvements (biking, walking, transit options)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward with the proposal by skipping the usual first reading step and planning to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance (city rule) being fast-tracked for approval
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading requirement waived to speed up process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve meeting minutes from their March sessions and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was mostly administrative housekeeping from previous meetings.
keyTopics: Approved multiple sets of meeting minutes from March 2026 sessions
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc. (details incomplete in agenda)
Routine administrative approvals

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and finalize their upcoming agendas for April 21 and April 28, 2026. The council reviewed what items should be added to each agenda, decided which topics needed specific time slots for discussion, and checked documents for proper legal notice requirements. The meeting also included a review of public records from early April and a report from the VEBA Advisory Committee.
keyTopics: Finalizing the April 21 council meeting agenda and adding any new items
Setting specific time slots for important agenda items to ensure they're discussed
Verifying proper legal notices were given for agenda items and documents
Organizing the April 28 draft agenda with new items and scheduling needs

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting to discuss the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including which infrastructure projects and improvements to fund. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in Mountain View.
keyTopics: Next year's budget for building and infrastructure projects
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
Planning session for capital improvements

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met on April 14, 2026 to review meeting notes from their previous session and discuss a new rule about tenant protections for renters in Sunnyvale. The council introduced changes to local housing laws that could affect how landlords and tenants interact.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
New tenant protection rules for residential renters
Changes to Chapter 19.71 of Sunnyvale Municipal Code

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where residents had the chance to speak directly to the council for up to 3 minutes about any topics they wanted to discuss. The council also conducted elections to choose new leadership positions and started work on the city's budget for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents could address the council on any matter
Election of new Chair and Vice Chair for the council
Budget planning discussions for the upcoming fiscal year
Reminder that council cannot make decisions on items not officially scheduled

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal disclosures about how residents can challenge city decisions in court. If you disagree with a city decision, you have 90 days from when that decision becomes final to file a lawsuit—if you wait longer, you'll lose your right to challenge it.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over decisions
Legal requirements for challenging quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 rules
When decision deadlines begin (becoming final)

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle routine business including updating the city's long-term housing and community development plans for 2025-2030, reviewing new project requests from individual council members, and potentially changing how the city fills council vacancies. The meeting also covered standard administrative items like approving previous meeting minutes and planning the calendar for upcoming meetings.
keyTopics: Update to the 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
New project requests from council members
Changes to how council vacancies are filled
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner wants to upgrade the building's historic status from Category 4 to Category 2, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of space for renovations and earthquake safety improvements.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade request
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Downtown Commercial zone development
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule approval
